title: rubus serratus
intro: Rubus serratus, also known as the Saw-leaf Bramble, is a species of thorny shrub that belongs to the rose family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus serratus include its ability to improve cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and aid in the treatment of digestive issues. Rubus serratus is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves and stems can also be used in herbal infusions and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
